the one in Miyazu, Kyoto|Kono Shrine#Okumiya (external sessha)}} {{神社 |名称 = Manai Shrine |画像 = [[File:Manai jinja.JPG|280px|]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}} Yamashiro-chō 84 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 17 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Manai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Manai Shrine''' (まないじんじゃ, 眞名井神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yamashiro-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Oi six shrines|Q11073594|WD=Q11073594|1=Ou district}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is enshrined southeast of Chausuyama, identified with “Kaminahi-no” of the Oi District section in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}}. === History === In the Oi District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', two shrines are listed: “Manai-sha” (a Shrine Bureau shrine) and “Matsuna-i-sha” (non–Shrine Bureau shrine). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' also records “Manai Shrine” in Oi District, Izumo Province. However, this shrine appears as “Izanaki-sha” in ''Izumo Fudoki-shō'' ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|WD=Q7651|1=1683}}, {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} 3) and as “Izanagi-sha” in ''Unyōshi'' ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2), so in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Izanagi-sha.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed “Manai Shrine.” It is said that rites of this shrine were handled by the Akigami family, shrine family of {{ill|lt=Kamosu Shrine|Q3192477|WD=Q3192477|1=Kamosu Shrine}}, who served as both priest and ritual-fire attendant, and that shrine buildings of both shrines were built in the same periodsAsakawa (2006), p.39. === Precincts === ; Haiden : The present building was erected in {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9). ; Honden : {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}. The current building was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2). It was designated a prefectural tangible cultural property in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. ; Matsuna-i Shrine : Precinct shrine. ===Cultural properties=== ===Shimane Prefecture designated cultural properties=== *Honden - designated in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. === Manai Falls === East of this shrine is a waterfall called “Manai Falls”Regarding Manai, in ancient usage “i” (well) could also mean “waterfall” (Seki (2006), p.189).. ''Unyōshi'' notes “Manai Kōjin” near this waterfall and identifies that place with the Manai-sha of the Fudoki. Water drawn from this basin is said to have been used from ancient times for the sacred-fire succession rite and Niiname-sai of the {{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11395856|WD=Q11395856|1=Izumo clan}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * Asakawa Shigeo, ''Nihon no Bijutsu No. 476 Izumo Taisha'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Shibundō|Q11613176|WD=Q11613176|1=Shibundo}} ISBN 4-7843-3476-9 * Seki Kazuhiko, ''Commentary on Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Takine Yoshiyuki, ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === External links === * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/200 Manai Shrine Honden] - [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto Shimane Whole Museum] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:島根県指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

the one in Miyazu, Kyoto|Kono Shrine#Okumiya (external sessha)}} {{神社 |名称 = Manai Shrine |画像 = [[File:Manai jinja.JPG|280px|]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}} Yamashiro-chō 84 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 17 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Manai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Manai Shrine''' (まないじんじゃ, 眞名井神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yamashiro-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Oi six shrines|Q11073594|WD=Q11073594|1=Ou district}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is enshrined southeast of Chausuyama, identified with “Kaminahi-no” of the Oi District section in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}}. === History === In the Oi District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', two shrines are listed: “Manai-sha” (a Shrine Bureau shrine) and “Matsuna-i-sha” (non–Shrine Bureau shrine). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' also records “Manai Shrine” in Oi District, Izumo Province. However, this shrine appears as “Izanaki-sha” in ''Izumo Fudoki-shō'' ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|WD=Q7651|1=1683}}, {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} 3) and as “Izanagi-sha” in ''Unyōshi'' ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2), so in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Izanagi-sha.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed “Manai Shrine.” It is said that rites of this shrine were handled by the Akigami family, shrine family of {{ill|lt=Kamosu Shrine|Q3192477|WD=Q3192477|1=Kamosu Shrine}}, who served as both priest and ritual-fire attendant, and that shrine buildings of both shrines were built in the same periodsAsakawa (2006), p.39. === Precincts === ; Haiden : The present building was erected in {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9). ; Honden : {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}. The current building was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2). It was designated a prefectural tangible cultural property in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. ; Matsuna-i Shrine : Precinct shrine. ===Cultural properties=== ===Shimane Prefecture designated cultural properties=== *Honden - designated in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. === Manai Falls === East of this shrine is a waterfall called “Manai Falls”Regarding Manai, in ancient usage “i” (well) could also mean “waterfall” (Seki (2006), p.189).. ''Unyōshi'' notes “Manai Kōjin” near this waterfall and identifies that place with the Manai-sha of the Fudoki. Water drawn from this basin is said to have been used from ancient times for the sacred-fire succession rite and Niiname-sai of the {{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11395856|WD=Q11395856|1=Izumo clan}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * Asakawa Shigeo, ''Nihon no Bijutsu No. 476 Izumo Taisha'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Shibundō|Q11613176|WD=Q11613176|1=Shibundo}} ISBN 4-7843-3476-9 * Seki Kazuhiko, ''Commentary on Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Takine Yoshiyuki, ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === External links === * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/200 Manai Shrine Honden] - [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto Shimane Whole Museum] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:島根県指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

the one in Miyazu, Kyoto|Kono Shrine#Okumiya (external sessha)}} {{神社 |名称 = Manai Shrine |画像 = [[File:Manai jinja.JPG|280px|]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}} Yamashiro-chō 84 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 17 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Manai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Manai Shrine''' (まないじんじゃ, 眞名井神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yamashiro-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Oi six shrines|Q11073594|WD=Q11073594|1=Ou district}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is enshrined southeast of Chausuyama, identified with “Kaminahi-no” of the Oi District section in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}}. === History === In the Oi District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', two shrines are listed: “Manai-sha” (a Shrine Bureau shrine) and “Matsuna-i-sha” (non–Shrine Bureau shrine). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' also records “Manai Shrine” in Oi District, Izumo Province. However, this shrine appears as “Izanaki-sha” in ''Izumo Fudoki-shō'' ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|WD=Q7651|1=1683}}, {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} 3) and as “Izanagi-sha” in ''Unyōshi'' ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2), so in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Izanagi-sha.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed “Manai Shrine.” It is said that rites of this shrine were handled by the Akigami family, shrine family of {{ill|lt=Kamosu Shrine|Q3192477|WD=Q3192477|1=Kamosu Shrine}}, who served as both priest and ritual-fire attendant, and that shrine buildings of both shrines were built in the same periodsAsakawa (2006), p.39. === Precincts === ; Haiden : The present building was erected in {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9). ; Honden : {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}. The current building was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2). It was designated a prefectural tangible cultural property in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. ; Matsuna-i Shrine : Precinct shrine. ===Cultural properties=== ===Shimane Prefecture designated cultural properties=== *Honden - designated in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. === Manai Falls === East of this shrine is a waterfall called “Manai Falls”Regarding Manai, in ancient usage “i” (well) could also mean “waterfall” (Seki (2006), p.189).. ''Unyōshi'' notes “Manai Kōjin” near this waterfall and identifies that place with the Manai-sha of the Fudoki. Water drawn from this basin is said to have been used from ancient times for the sacred-fire succession rite and Niiname-sai of the {{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11395856|WD=Q11395856|1=Izumo clan}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * Asakawa Shigeo, ''Nihon no Bijutsu No. 476 Izumo Taisha'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Shibundō|Q11613176|WD=Q11613176|1=Shibundo}} ISBN 4-7843-3476-9 * Seki Kazuhiko, ''Commentary on Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Takine Yoshiyuki, ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === External links === * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/200 Manai Shrine Honden] - [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto Shimane Whole Museum] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

However, this shrine appears as “Izanaki-sha” in ''Izumo Fudoki-shō'' ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|WD=Q7651|1=1683}}, {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} 3) and as “Izanagi-sha” in ''Unyōshi'' ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2), so in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Izanagi-sha.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed “Manai Shrine.” It is said that rites of this shrine were handled by the Akigami family, shrine family of {{ill|lt=Kamosu Shrine|Q3192477|WD=Q3192477|1=Kamosu Shrine}}, who served as both priest and ritual-fire attendant, and that shrine buildings of both shrines were built in the same periodsAsakawa (2006), p.39. === Precincts === ; Haiden : The present building was erected in {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9). ; Honden : {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}. The current building was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2). It was designated a prefectural tangible cultural property in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. ; Matsuna-i Shrine : Precinct shrine. ===Cultural properties=== ===Shimane Prefecture designated cultural properties=== *Honden - designated in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. === Manai Falls === East of this shrine is a waterfall called “Manai Falls”Regarding Manai, in ancient usage “i” (well) could also mean “waterfall” (Seki (2006), p.189).. ''Unyōshi'' notes “Manai Kōjin” near this waterfall and identifies that place with the Manai-sha of the Fudoki. Water drawn from this basin is said to have been used from ancient times for the sacred-fire succession rite and Niiname-sai of the {{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11395856|WD=Q11395856|1=Izumo clan}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * Asakawa Shigeo, ''Nihon no Bijutsu No. 476 Izumo Taisha'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Shibundō|Q11613176|WD=Q11613176|1=Shibundo}} ISBN 4-7843-3476-9 * Seki Kazuhiko, ''Commentary on Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Takine Yoshiyuki, ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === External links === * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/200 Manai Shrine Honden] - [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto Shimane Whole Museum] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

the one in Miyazu, Kyoto|Kono Shrine#Okumiya (external sessha)}} {{神社 |名称 = Manai Shrine |画像 = [[File:Manai jinja.JPG|280px|]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}} Yamashiro-chō 84 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 17 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Manai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Manai Shrine''' (まないじんじゃ, 眞名井神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yamashiro-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|WD=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Oi six shrines|Q11073594|WD=Q11073594|1=Ou district}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is enshrined southeast of Chausuyama, identified with “Kaminahi-no” of the Oi District section in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}}. === History === In the Oi District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', two shrines are listed: “Manai-sha” (a Shrine Bureau shrine) and “Matsuna-i-sha” (non–Shrine Bureau shrine). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' also records “Manai Shrine” in Oi District, Izumo Province. However, this shrine appears as “Izanaki-sha” in ''Izumo Fudoki-shō'' ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|WD=Q7651|1=1683}}, {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|WD=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} 3) and as “Izanagi-sha” in ''Unyōshi'' ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2), so in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Izanagi-sha.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed “Manai Shrine.” It is said that rites of this shrine were handled by the Akigami family, shrine family of {{ill|lt=Kamosu Shrine|Q3192477|WD=Q3192477|1=Kamosu Shrine}}, who served as both priest and ritual-fire attendant, and that shrine buildings of both shrines were built in the same periodsAsakawa (2006), p.39. === Precincts === ; Haiden : The present building was erected in {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9). ; Honden : {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}. The current building was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|WD=Q7574|1=1662}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2). It was designated a prefectural tangible cultural property in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. ; Matsuna-i Shrine : Precinct shrine. ===Cultural properties=== ===Shimane Prefecture designated cultural properties=== *Honden - designated in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|WD=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. === Manai Falls === East of this shrine is a waterfall called “Manai Falls”Regarding Manai, in ancient usage “i” (well) could also mean “waterfall” (Seki (2006), p.189).. ''Unyōshi'' notes “Manai Kōjin” near this waterfall and identifies that place with the Manai-sha of the Fudoki. Water drawn from this basin is said to have been used from ancient times for the sacred-fire succession rite and Niiname-sai of the {{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11395856|WD=Q11395856|1=Izumo clan}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * Asakawa Shigeo, ''Nihon no Bijutsu No. 476 Izumo Taisha'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Shibundō|Q11613176|WD=Q11613176|1=Shibundo}} ISBN 4-7843-3476-9 * Seki Kazuhiko, ''Commentary on Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|WD=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Takine Yoshiyuki, ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|WD=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === External links === * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/200 Manai Shrine Honden] - [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto Shimane Whole Museum] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|qid=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = October 17 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Manai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Manai Shrine''' (まないじんじゃ, 眞名井神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yamashiro-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Oi six shrines|Q11073594|qid=Q11073594|1=Ou district}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is enshrined southeast of Chausuyama, identified with “Kaminahi-no” of the Oi District section in the {{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|qid=Q10940228|lt=Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}}. === History === In the Oi District section of ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', two shrines are listed: “Manai-sha” (a Shrine Bureau shrine) and “Matsuna-i-sha” (non–Shrine Bureau shrine). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' also records “Manai Shrine” in Oi District, Izumo Province. However, this shrine appears as “Izanaki-sha” in ''Izumo Fudoki-shō'' ({{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|qid=Q7651|1=1683}}, {{ill|lt=Tenna|Q1145289|qid=Q1145289|1=Tenna}} 3) and as “Izanagi-sha” in ''Unyōshi'' ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|qid=Q6895|1=1717}}, {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2), so in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Izanagi-sha.” In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was renamed “Manai Shrine.” It is said that rites of this shrine were handled by the Akigami family, shrine family of {{ill|lt=Kamosu Shrine|Q3192477|qid=Q3192477|1=Kamosu Shrine}}, who served as both priest and ritual-fire attendant, and that shrine buildings of both shrines were built in the same periodsAsakawa (2006), p.39. === Precincts === ; Haiden : The present building was erected in {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|qid=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9). ; Honden : {{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|qid=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}}. The current building was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|qid=Q7574|1=1662}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2). It was designated a prefectural tangible cultural property in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|qid=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. ; Matsuna-i Shrine : Precinct shrine. ===Cultural properties=== ===Shimane Prefecture designated cultural properties=== *Honden - designated in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2072|qid=Q2072|1=Marcel Hirscher}}. === Manai Falls === East of this shrine is a waterfall called “Manai Falls”Regarding Manai, in ancient usage “i” (well) could also mean “waterfall” (Seki (2006), p.189).. ''Unyōshi'' notes “Manai Kōjin” near this waterfall and identifies that place with the Manai-sha of the Fudoki. Water drawn from this basin is said to have been used from ancient times for the sacred-fire succession rite and Niiname-sai of the {{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11395856|qid=Q11395856|1=Izumo clan}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * Asakawa Shigeo, ''Nihon no Bijutsu No. 476 Izumo Taisha'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Shibundō|Q11613176|qid=Q11613176|1=Shibundo}} ISBN 4-7843-3476-9 * Seki Kazuhiko, ''Commentary on Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|qid=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Takine Yoshiyuki, ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|qid=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 === External links === * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/200 Manai Shrine Honden] - [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto Shimane Whole Museum] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Manai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shimane Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]
